content: We can borrow the graded, scaffolded reflection practice from high school English language arts lessons (where 'reflexion' is taught as both a linguistic term and a critical thinking habit) to design progressive self-reflection fine-tuning pipelines for large language models. Just as students build reflective writing skills from simple word meaning reflection to structured critical self-analysis of their own arguments, LLMs can be trained to move from basic output consistency checks to multi-perspective critique of their own reasoning.
